proposals.title: Fix health check logic inconsistency in recordError
proposals.rationale: In `recordSuccess`, the health check allows `lastErrorTime === 0` (no errors yet) to be healthy, but `recordError` always sets `lastErrorTime = Date.now()` before the check, so the `> 30000` condition is always false immediately after an error. More critically, `recordError` omits the `lastErrorTime === 0` fallback that `recordSuccess` has, creating an inconsistency. The real bug is that a single error immediately marks a provider unhealthy regardless of error rate, since `Date.now() - m.lastErrorTime` will be ~0. The health check should primarily rely on error rate, with a cooldown only for recently-errored providers.

proposals.title: Improve readability of fallbackSummary method
proposals.rationale: The fallbackSummary method uses a complex regex to extract file paths, which is hard to read and maintain. Replacing it with a simpler approach using JSON.parse improves clarity and reduces the risk of regex errors.

proposals.title: Simplify version string to single source of truth
proposals.rationale: The manifest hardcodes '6.15.0' in generateManifest() while the system prompt addendum says 'v6.13', creating inconsistency. Extracting the version to a constant ensures alignment and easier maintenance.
proposals.diff:
===================================================================
--- a/manifest.ts
+++ b/manifest.ts
@@ -361,7 +361,7 @@
   });
 
   return {
-    version: "6.15.0",
+    version: APP_VERSION,
     identity: "Andromeda",
     generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
     sections: allSections,

proposals.title: Improve readability of diversity scoring logic
proposals.rationale: The diversity scoring logic is currently a single monolithic function with inline calculations, making it hard to follow and maintain. Extracting the score calculation into a helper function and using clearer variable names improves readability without changing behavior.

proposals.title: Extract eviction logic into a dedicated function
proposals.rationale: The publish function contains inline eviction logic that mixes concerns and reduces readability. Extracting it into a separate `evictOldEntries` function clarifies the publish flow and makes the eviction policy easier to test and modify independently.

proposals.title: Improve readability by consolidating workspace directory logic
proposals.rationale: The getWorkspaceDir function duplicates the logic for resolving the server directory and creating the workspace, which is already handled in getServerDir. By reusing getServerDir, the code becomes more readable and maintainable, reducing redundancy.

proposals.title: Add JSDoc re-export comments for clarity
proposals.rationale: Adding JSDoc comments to each re-export line documents the purpose of each sub-module directly in the barrel file, improving readability for developers scanning the exports. This makes it immediately clear what each module provides without needing to open separate files.
proposals.diff:
===================================================================
--- a/ai.ts
+++ b/ai.ts
@@ -13,7 +13,11 @@
  * to work without modification.
  */
 
+/** Token counting and model/provider helpers */
 export * from "./aiTokens.js";
+/** System prompt builders */
 export * from "./aiPrompts.js";
+/** Core SSE streaming engine and public streaming API */
 export * from "./aiStreaming.js";
+/** Agent planning, Claude Code capabilities, todo system */
 export * from "./aiPlanning.js";
proposals.originalContent:
/**
 * ai.ts — v6.25 (barrel export)
 *
 * This file is now a thin barrel that re-exports everything from the focused
 * sub-modules created in the v6.25 god-module split:
 *
 *   aiTokens.ts    — Token counting and model/provider helpers
 *   aiPrompts.ts   — System prompt builders
 *   aiStreaming.ts — Core SSE streaming engine and public streaming API
 *   aiPlanning.ts  — Agent planning, Claude Code capabilities, todo system
 *
 * All existing import paths (e.g. `import { streamChat } from "./ai"`) continue
 * to work without modification.
 */

export * from "./aiTokens.js";
export * from "./aiPrompts.js";
export * from "./aiStreaming.js";
export * from "./aiPlanning.js";

proposals.title: Extract provider config into separate module
proposals.rationale: The file mixes static provider definitions with runtime logic, making it hard to maintain and test. Extracting DEFAULT_PROVIDERS into a dedicated config file improves separation of concerns and allows providers to be updated independently.
proposals.proposedSnippet:
// DEFAULT_PROVIDERS moved to ./providers.ts
import { DEFAULT_PROVIDERS } from "./providers.js";

proposals.title: Extract inline routes into separate modules
proposals.rationale: The file contains over 20 inline route handlers, making it difficult to maintain and test. Extracting each route group into dedicated modules (e.g., searchRoutes, chatRoutes, workspaceRoutes) would improve modularity, testability, and readability.

proposals.title: Remove unused DEEPSEEK_API_KEY check
proposals.rationale: The runTeamAgent function checks for process.env.DEEPSEEK_API_KEY but the runAgent function no longer uses it, having switched to an active provider via llmProvider. Removing this check eliminates a dead code path and potential confusion about required environment variables.
proposals.proposedSnippet:
// API key is now managed by the LLM provider; no need to check here.
  const apiKey = "";
